section_title: INTRODUCTION TO SHORT-TERM TRADING AND LOW RESISTANCE LIQUIDITY RUNS
content:
This lesson focuses on short-term trading, specifically addressing low resistance liquidity runs and
consolidations.
FOUNDATION:

section_title: TIME AND PRICE IN TRADING SETUPS
content:
Every trading setup is predicated on time and price. The time element is related to the 'up-to-date ranges':
- Last 60 days
- Last 40 days
- Last 20 days
We concentrate on premium and discount PD (Price Delivery) arrays. For short-term trading, the look-back
period is the last three months, specifically the 60-day up-to-date range. This period comprises all
necessary elements to frame a short-term trade, or 'one-shot, one-kill' scenario.

section_title: PRICE MOVEMENT BETWEEN PREMIUM AND DISCOUNT
content:
Price moves between a premium array and a discount array, or vice versa. Within these two realms, price
will key off a time element linked to the up-to-date range. This approach blends both time and price
elements (20, 40, and 60 days) with institutional reference points in the PD array Matrix.

section_title: BRITISH POUND USD (CABLE)
content:
- DAILY CHART ANALYSIS
For this study, we are using the British Pound USD (Cable) on a daily chart. The first step is to define
whether the market is trending or consolidating. In the case of the British Pound, the market has been in
consolidation, not trending.

section_title: DEFINING THE CONSOLIDATION RANGE AND LOOK-BACK PERIOD
content:
When a market is range-bound and cannot move outside its current trading range on a daily chart, we
frame this over approximately three months, or 60 trading days. This 60-day period is the relative 'up-to
- date range' used for maximum look-back definition.
To define the range, we identify its highest and lowest candlestick bodies, not focusing on the wicks. We
look for the highest and lowest open or close within the last 60 trading days to establish the defined
consolidation range.

section_title: IDENTIFYING THE RANGE MIDPOINT (50%) USING FIBONACCI
content:
We use the Fibonacci tool, available on every platform, to determine the 50% midpoint of the defined
consolidation range.
- Drag the Fib tool from the highest body candle (highest open or close) to the lowest body candle (lowest
open or close) within the last 60 trading days.
- Do not use the highest or lowest wick.
- Note the 50% level and draw a horizontal line on the daily chart at this midpoint.

section_title: UPPER AND LOWER 50% LEVELS
content:
Next, we determine the 50% levels of the upper and lower halves of the main consolidation range:
- Above the main 50% level: This is our 'premium' area, where we look for sell scenarios or taking profit on
long positions.
- Below the main 50% level: This is our 'discount' area, where we look for long positions or covering short
positions.

section_title: MEASURING SUB-RANGES WITH FIBONACCI
content:
1. Upper Range Subdivision: Measure with the Fib tool from the highest body close/open down to the main
consolidation midpoint. Note its 50% level and draw a horizontal line.
2. Lower Range Subdivision: Measure with the Fib tool from the midpoint of the total consolidation range
down to the lowest body close/open. Note its 50% level and draw a horizontal line.

section_title: IDENTIFYING PREMIUM AND DISCOUNT IN SUB-RANGES
content:
By defining the consolidation range in this manner, we create a clear identification of premium and discount
zones:
- Anything above the main midpoint (50% level, often shaded gray) is considered a 'premium range'.
- Anything below the main midpoint is considered a 'discount range'.

section_title: APPLYING PD ARRAYS ACROSS TIMEFRAMES
content:
We then identify PD arrays (Price Delivery arrays) within each newly defined range. This involves applying
relevant monthly, weekly, and daily PD arrays to the chart.
Every premium and discount array provides setups within the overall PD array Matrix. We look for a blend
of time and price to create a setup for a buy or a sell.

section_title: HIGH PROBABILITY TRADE SETUPS IN CONSOLIDATION
content:
For short positions, the highest probability trades involve looking for any PD array in the premium range of
the consolidation (anything above the main midpoint/50% gray level). These are ideal sell scenarios when
coupled with a premium PD array.

section_title: BLENDING TIME AND PRICE FOR SHORT SCENARIOS
content:
We blend time and price, looking for a sell move to correlate to a later discount PD array. Price typically
moves down to either:
- The 50% level of the total consolidation.
- Any level below the midpoint or 50% level of the consolidation.
Example: A fair value gap and bearish order block in the premium level (around February 2017) paired with
a weekly bullish order block and threshold in a discount (in March 2017).

section_title: FURTHER QUADRANT DEFINITION FOR PREMIUM AND DISCOUNT
content:
The initial premium and discount quadrants can be further subdivided into their own respective unique
premium and discount trading ranges. This means dividing each quadrant into equal halves, which can
then be viewed from a premium and discount format themselves.

section_title: LOWER QUADRANT TRADING OPPORTUNITIES
content:
In the lower quadrant (discount range of the overall consolidation), when elements form as a discount PD
array in a discount sub-range:
- These are high probability short covering objectives.
- They are also high probability new long positions.
- First profit on a long is best taken once price enters the premium range of this sub-PD array Matrix.

section_title: UPPER QUADRANT TRADING OPPORTUNITIES
content:
The same principle applies to the upper quadrants (premium range of the overall consolidation):
- Ideal long positions are entered at the discount level or in that discount sub-range.
- Taking first profit is best applied by exiting the long position in the premium range of this sub-PD array
Matrix.

section_title: LOWER TIMEFRAME TARGETS FOR FIRST OBJECTIVES
content:
On lower timeframes like the 4-hour or 1-hour chart, you will find premium PD arrays. These can be paired
with your daily analysis for first objectives and profit targets.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC DEFINITION OF PREMIUM AND DISCOUNT THROUGH QUADRANTS
content:
Each half of the overall range is further divided into new halves, defining premium and discount in an
algorithmic way. When markets create discounts in these sub-ranges, they represent high probability long
entries.

section_title: LONG ENTRIES IN DISCOUNT SUB-RANGES AND PROFIT TAKING
content:
For longs, a 4-hour or 1-hour premium PD array (not visible on the daily chart) would be the first profit
objective. This means you would drop down to a lower timeframe to identify these premium arrays for
exiting longs that were entered by buying in the discount area.

section_title: TRADING LONGS WITHIN AN OVERALL PREMIUM MARKET
content:
Even when price is above the midpoint of the total consolidation range (technically in an overall premium
market), longs are still possible. This is because the market is subdivided: if you're in a lower portion of that
larger premium range (i.e., a discount within that premium sub-range), there is still room to reach for higher
premium PD arrays. This means it's a 'discount market' in an 'overall premium market' that still has room to
go up.

section_title: OVERSOLD/UNDERVALUED VS. OVERBOUGHT/OVERVALUED
content:
This method avoids the limitations of indicators for defining overbought/oversold conditions. By looking at
price ranges algorithmically through quadrants, we gain a greater definition on how to frame entries and
classify them:
- Oversold or undervalued entries: For long positions.
- Overvalued or overbought entries: For short positions.
Example: Buying at a daily bullish order block mean threshold and taking profit when price trades up into a
4-hour or 1-hour premium PD array in the premium range.

section_title: COUPLING RANGE DEFINITION WITH PD ARRAYS
content:
As we move further up the consolidation range, new discount and premium PD array matrices are defined.
We are coupling the range definition (premium/discount) with actual PD arrays such as:
- Fair value gaps
- Bearish order blocks
- Weekly rejection blocks
These elements are identified by looking back 20, 40, and 60 trading days for the 'up-to-date' range.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC MARKET VIEW
content:
- NOT INDICATOR BASED
This approach blends time and price, viewing the market in terms of overbought/oversold, but not in the
context of an indicator. Instead, it's an algorithmic perspective based on quadrants broken down into
premium and discount.
By itself, a quadrant doesn't mean anything. However, when price trades into a PD array (discount or
premium), whether it's a bullish order block, a breaker, or any other element from the PD array Matrix, it
provides a high probability setup.

section_title: HIGH PROBABILITY ENTRIES AND EXITS IN CONSOLIDATION
content:
- Longs: Highest probable entries are in the discount range, coupled with a premium range PD array for
exits.
- Anticipating Oscillation: In consolidation, price can oscillate, moving slightly outside the range before
returning. This method anticipates this oscillation, finding smaller consolidations within the larger one,
allowing for overbought/oversold analysis without indicators.

section_title: FRAMING OVERSOLD CONDITIONS IN A PREMIUM MARKET
content:
Even when in the upper half (premium portion) of a larger consolidation, an 'oversold condition' can be
framed. This is a discount market within an overall premium market that still has room to move up. This
defines entries for longs (undervalued) or shorts (overvalued).

section_title: ELEMENTS FOR FRAMING LONG ENTRIES AND PROFIT TAKING
content:
For entries and exits, we look for elements that frame discount PD arrays:
- Bullish order blocks
- Breakers
- Fair value gaps below price
- Price trading below an old low to take out sell stops
These ideas frame long positions. First profits are taken on a 4-hour or 1-hour basis once price enters a
premium PD array Matrix range.

section_title: PREMIUM MARKET INFLUENCE AND OPPORTUNITIES FOR SHORTS
content:
At the top of the larger consolidation, opportunities for buyers fall off significantly in terms of probabilities. In
this highest segment, the premium PD array Matrix (bearish order blocks, old highs, bearish breakers,
bearish mitigation blocks) becomes much more influential, leading to dynamic price action.

section_title: EXAMPLE OF STRONG REJECTION IN PREMIUM RANGE
content:
When price reaches the last smallest portion of the premium market or premium PD array Matrix (the
shaded premium area), it will have a much more impactful response. For instance, a weekly rejection block,
also taking out an old high (February 2017), caused a significant sell-off after hitting that final midpoint
dividing premium and discount.

section_title: PRICE RESPONSE AND TARGETING
content:
Following the sell-off, price traded down into a daily bullish order block mean threshold, leading to a 100
- pip move. This rally then traded right back up to a daily bearish order block, overlapping with the premium
50% level, illustrating the interplay of these concepts.

section_title: IMPORTANCE OF PERSONAL STUDY AND IDENTIFYING LOW RESISTANCE LIQUIDITY
content:
RUNS
This framework requires personal study and breaking down charts. You must actively look for these
opportunities. The goal is to identify low resistance liquidity runs from one PD array to another, moving from
a discount to a premium. This is coupled with a look-back of the last three months, as 'one-shot, one-kill'
excels within this timeframe (20, 40, and 60 trading days).

section_title: CONTEXTUALIZING ENTRIES AND OBJECTIVES WITH PD ARRAY MATRIX
content:
The 20, 40, and 60-day look-back periods provide the framework to determine:
- Where the premium PD array Matrix leads for selling targets.
- Where discount PD array Matrix levels are for covering shorts or finding long entries and exits.
Applying these ranges (premium/discount market) builds context for entry and first objective targeting.

section_title: IDEAL BUY AND SELL ZONES
content:
- Best Buys: In an ideal world, the best buys are in the lower portion or lower half of the overall
consolidation, specifically in the lower quadrant (the discount Matrix).
- Highest Probability Sells or Long Exits: These are in the premium range, specifically in the upper portion
(the last quadrant). This is where the algorithm will reach for selling or exiting longs.

section_title: NAVIGATING OVERBOUGHT ZONES
content:
Any long position taken in a premium range must be approached with caution, understanding the overall
range. Even in an overall premium zone, if you identify a daily bullish order block mean threshold, a 100-pip
rally can occur. This rally, while originating in a discount within that premium, trades up to a daily bearish
order block in the premium range, providing a high probability target.

section_title: DEFINITION OF 'ONE SHOT ONE KILL'
content:
One shot one kill is not defined by getting in on a Monday and getting out on a Friday. It refers to short-term
trading, which can be as little as one day and as long as a week.

section_title: AVOIDING THE 'CHOP ZONE' (MIDDLE RANGE)
content:
The middle range of the consolidation is where you are most likely to get 'chopped up' (experience
whipsaws and losses). This happens if:
- The range is defined incorrectly.
- You are looking at premium or discount PD arrays that have already been traded to.
We want to use new PD arrays in a discount or premium market that have not been traded to. The
algorithm knows when price has been there before and will seek new liquidity, expanding to allow banks to
participate in a move.

section_title: SUMMARY OF OPTIMAL TRADING AREAS AND LOW RESISTANCE LIQUIDITY RUNS
content:
- For Shorts: The higher you get up in the overall consolidation within a premium market, the better your
short positions will be. It will be easier to reach for a profitable discount PD array.
- For Longs: The lower you are in the total consolidation, the better your discount long entries will be. It will
be easier for you to get to a profitable premium PD array.
Blending time and price in a range environment provides context, allowing you to find very easy low
resistance liquidity runs between one PD array and another.
